# claude_federation_rule (Resource)

A Workload Identity Federation rule (`fdrl_...`): binds an issuer to a service account so that JWTs satisfying the match conditions can mint tokens for the target.

OAuth-authenticated callers may only manage rules whose `oauth_scope` is `workspace:developer` or `workspace:inference`; other scopes (such as `org:admin`) must be managed in the Console.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# GitHub Actions deploys from the main branch act as a service account.
resource "claude_federation_rule" "gha_deploy" {
  name       = "gha-deploy"
  issuer_id  = claude_federation_issuer.github_actions.id
  oauth_scope = "workspace:developer"

  match = {
    subject_prefix = "repo:my-org/my-repo:ref:refs/heads/main"
    claims = {
      repository_owner = "my-org"
    }
  }

  target = {
    service_account_id = claude_service_account.inference_worker.id
  }

  workspace_id           = "wrkspc_..."
  token_lifetime_seconds = 600
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `issuer_id` (String) Federation issuer (`fdis_...`) whose tokens this rule accepts. Changing this forces a new rule, as a rule's issuer cannot be changed.
- `match` (Attributes) Conditions the verified JWT must satisfy. At least one of `subject_prefix`, `claims`, or `condition` is required. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--match))
- `name` (String) Slug identifier. Must match `^[a-z0-9-]+$`, be 1 to 255 characters, and be unique within the organization.
- `oauth_scope` (String) Space-separated OAuth scopes granted on the minted token, e.g. `workspace:developer` (the typical default) or `workspace:inference`.
- `target` (Attributes) Identity that tokens minted via this rule act as. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--target))

### Optional

- `applies_to_all_workspaces` (Boolean) Enable this rule for every workspace in the org (including ones created later). Either this or `workspace_id` must be set.
- `description` (String) Optional free-text description.
- `token_lifetime_seconds` (Number) Lifetime in seconds of tokens minted via this rule (60-86400). Defaults to 3600.
- `workspace_id` (String) Workspace (`wrkspc_...`) to enable this rule for at creation. Required unless `applies_to_all_workspaces` is true. Changing it forces a new rule.

### Read-Only

- `archived_at` (String) RFC 3339 timestamp of when the rule was archived, if archived.
- `created_at` (String) RFC 3339 timestamp of when the rule was created.
- `id` (String) Identifier of the federation rule (`fdrl_...`).
- `issuer_name` (String) Issuer's display name at read time.
- `type` (String) Object type, always `federation_rule`.
- `updated_at` (String) RFC 3339 timestamp of when the rule was last updated.
- `workspace_ids` (List of String) Workspaces this rule is enabled for.

<a id="nestedatt--match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `match`

Optional:

- `audience` (String) Exact match against the `aud` claim. Overrides the issuer's default audience.
- `claims` (Map of String) Exact-match `{claim: value}` pairs against top-level string claims.
- `condition` (String) CEL expression over the `claims` variable for logic the structural fields cannot express.
- `subject_prefix` (String) Match against the JWT `sub` claim. Exact match unless it ends with `*`, which makes it a prefix match.


<a id="nestedatt--target"></a>
### Nested Schema for `target`

Required:

- `service_account_id` (String) Service account (`svac_...`) to mint tokens for.

Optional:

- `type` (String) Target type, always `service_account`.

Read-Only:

- `service_account_name` (String) Service account's display name at read time.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Federation rules can be imported by their fdrl_ identifier.
terraform import claude_federation_rule.gha_deploy fdrl_0123456789abcdef
```
